WHY EACH MCOWhy each MCO needs Mission Mobile
Sunflower — largest panel, warmest entry
Largest KanCare market share (~141K+ members) = the most UTR members in rural Kansas. Every 50 UTR members activated = $210K/yr in withhold revenue.
Centene warm entry — existing enterprise relationship (Sunshine Health FL, PAHW) makes this the fastest SOW path of the three MCOs.
Withhold recovery math is immediate — Kansas withholds 1–5% of capitation. A 2% withhold on Sunflower's Kansas capitation is significant six-to-seven figure annual exposure.
Proven reference parallel — Centene's Sunshine Health (FL, $2M) and PAHW are live proof points. Use these to eliminate the 'prove it works in our state' objection.
Healthy Blue — 2027 reprocurement risk
New entrant Jan 2025 — no track record with KDHE yet. Contract ends Dec 2027. Rural access is an explicit KDHE evaluator criterion.
Mission Mobile is their field-operational proof point — not a plan, an operating program in frontier counties KDHE can see before the 2027 bid.
One parent, two conversations — Anthem/Elevance also operates D-SNP Medicare Advantage in Kansas. One parent, two separate deals.
New CMO (Dr. Knolla, Aug 2025) is actively building clinical programs — perfect window for CMO-to-CMO gap closure conversation.
UHC — two buying centers, one enterprise
Operates both KanCare Medicaid AND Medicare Advantage in Kansas — two separate deals from one enterprise relationship.
AWV + STAR uplift is the cleanest MA entry. Frontier seniors aren't getting annual wellness visits. Mobile brings the AWV to them.
Dual-eligible sweet spot — frontier Kansas skews older. Same members appear in both KanCare and MA plans. One deployment, double value.
Activating UTR members improves RAF scores (more capitation) AND quality measures simultaneously — double win at both buying centers.
Magellan Complete Care KS — behavioral health + LTSS specialization
Magellan Complete Care focuses on high-need behavioral health and LTSS KanCare members — the highest-cost, most unreachable population.
CHW field engagement is particularly critical for behavioral health members who disengage from care systems.
Mobile clinic closes physical health HEDIS gaps (CBP, HbA1c) for members whose behavioral health needs dominate care interactions.
LTSS focus creates unique opportunity for mobile home-based care model alongside CHW visits.
QUALITY MEASURESQuality measure priorities — side by side
Quality measure Sunflower Health PlanHealthy Blue KansasUnitedHealthcareMagellan Complete Care of Kansas
CBP — Blood pressure control
★★★ ★★★ PRIMARY
Triple-weighted in KS state contract. Controlling HTN in 118 patients saves $325K/yr per 2,000 members.
★★★ ★★★ PRIMARY
Triple-weighted. New entrant must prove quality to KDHE before 2027 renewal. CBP is the highest-value single measure to move.
★★★ ★★★ PRIMARY
Applies to both Medicaid HEDIS and MA STAR. Dual-program impact makes CBP the top cross-MCO Kiowa measure.
★★★ ★★★ PRIMARY
Triple-weighted in state contract. HTN control is the highest-value single measure to move.
HbA1c — Glycemic control
★★★ ★★★ PRIMARY
1% improvement = $555/patient/yr. Slowing CKD 30% saves $1.3M/yr per 2,000 members. High in frontier KS.
★★ ★★ HIGH
Frontier KS has high uncontrolled diabetes. Demonstrable clinically and financially for a new entrant.
★★★ ★★★ PRIMARY
Key MA STAR measure and Medicaid HEDIS metric. $555/patient/yr improvement across dual-eligible panel.
★★★ ★★★ PRIMARY
1% improvement = $555/patient/yr. Mobile A1c monitoring at scale across rural panel.
UTR Activation
★★★ ★★★ PRIMARY
Most UTR members of any KS MCO. $210K/yr per 50 activated. CHWs find members call centers cannot.
★★★ ★★★ PRIMARY
Activating unreachable frontier members is the most visible proof point for KDHE reprocurement evaluation.
★★★ ★★★ PRIMARY
Improves RAF scores (more capitation) AND quality simultaneously — double win at both buying centers.
★★★ ★★★ PRIMARY
$210K/yr per 50 UTR members activated. CHWs find members call centers cannot reach.
PPC — Prenatal & postpartum
★★ ★★ HIGH
Low birth weight infant = $30K+ NICU. CHWs track pregnant members and schedule mobile visits.
★★★ ★★★ PRIMARY
Maternal mortality is a top KDHE state priority. Showing strong PPC signals KDHE alignment for a new entrant.
★ ★ MODERATE
Less central to the MA pitch. Relevant on Medicaid — keep in program scope, not primary MA angle.
★★ ★★ HIGH
CHWs track pregnant members and schedule mobile prenatal/postpartum visits.
ED Utilization
★★ ★★ HIGH
61% of rural Medicaid ED visits are avoidable at $1,800 each vs. $200 primary care.
★★ ★★ HIGH
New entrant is financially exposed. Preventing avoidable ED visits reduces cost while proving access effectiveness.
★★ ★★ HIGH
MA cost control priority. Frontier areas have no urgent care alternative — mobile prevents unnecessary ED.
★★ ★★ HIGH
61% of rural Medicaid ED visits are avoidable. Mobile reduces $1,800 ED to $200 primary care.
Readmissions
★ ★ MODERATE
CHF readmission = $25K+. Post-discharge CHW follow-up + medication reconciliation via mobile.
★ ★ MODERATE
Less urgent given near-term focus on access proof points for KDHE. Secondary measure.
★★ ★★ HIGH
MA readmission penalties apply. Post-discharge CHW follow-up particularly relevant for senior dual-eligibles.
★ ★ MODERATE
Post-discharge CHW follow-up prevents $25K+ readmissions for rural members.

30-SECOND PITCH30-second pitch — memorize the one for your MCO
Pitch for Sunflower Health Plan
"You have the largest Medicaid panel in Kansas — and the most unreachable members in frontier counties. We're already rotating mobile clinics through your KanCare geography. Our CHWs find and engage the UTR members your call center can't. Plans pay us a base fee plus performance for HEDIS improvement — and the model is self-funding once running. I want to show you the withhold math on your Kansas numbers."
Pitch for Healthy Blue Kansas
"You launched as the only new KanCare entrant in January 2025, and your contract comes up for renewal in 2027. Rural access is one of the criteria KDHE evaluates. Kiowa County Hospital is already running a mobile clinic rotating through your frontier KanCare geography. We don't want to sell you a plan. We want to give you an operational proof point for your KDHE relationship before that bid window opens."
Pitch for UnitedHealthcare
"Jake — the frontier AWV program is live. We're operating mobile clinics in rural Kansas ZIP codes where your MA members are missing annual wellness visits. I want to show you the STAR measure math on your Kansas panel. If we move AWV completion from 32% to 55% in the Kiowa catchment, that's meaningful STAR impact — and we have the operating model to do it. Same program also creates Medicaid HEDIS value for Kevin Sparks on the KanCare side."
Pitch for Magellan Complete Care of Kansas
"Magellan Complete Care of Kansas has significant Medicaid enrollment with unreachable members in rural areas. Our CHWs engage the UTR members your call center can't find. Plans pay us a base fee plus performance for HEDIS improvement — and the model is self-funding once running. I want to show you the withhold math on your state numbers."
